originalTitle: Sudan accuses Ethiopia and UAE of orchestrating drone attacks on airport
title: Sudan Says Big Countries Are Not Being Friends!
content:
Hello, everyone! Your friendly news reporter from Africa here. Today, we have some important news from a country called Sudan. Sometimes, countries have big talks, and sometimes they have disagreements, just like friends do. Let's learn about something that made Sudan feel very sad and worried. We will explore what happened and learn about the countries involved. It's like a big puzzle we need to solve together!

Sudan Has Something Important to Say

Sudan is a very big country in Africa. The special talker for Sudan, called the foreign minister, said something important. He said that two other countries, Ethiopia and the UAE, did something bad. This made Sudan very upset. Sudan says these countries sent special flying robots, called drones, to attack their airport. An airport is a very busy place where big planes take off and land. Attacking an airport is a serious problem because it can stop people from traveling and make things unsafe.

What is an Airport?

Did you know? An airport is a super important place! It's like a big house for aeroplanes. People go to airports to catch planes to fly to other cities or even other countries. Lots of people work there to make sure planes fly safely and on time. Airports help people visit their families, go on holidays, and even send important packages. So, when an airport is attacked, it causes many problems for everyone. It stops people from doing what they need to do.

Let's Learn About Drones!

Did you know? Drones are like big, clever flying toys! Some drones are small and fun, and you can buy them in shops. But some drones are very big and strong. They fly high in the sky without a pilot sitting inside them. A person on the ground controls them with a special remote. Sudan says that big, serious drones were used to attack their airport. This kind of drone is not for playing. It can cause damage and make people scared. It's a serious tool, not a toy for fun games.

Meet Sudan: A Country in Africa!

Sudan is a really big country right here in Africa. It has long, winding rivers and very old stories. Did you know? Sudan is famous for its pyramids, just like Egypt! These pyramids are thousands of years old and are amazing to see. Many visitors come to Sudan to see them. Sudan wants to live peacefully with all its neighbours and friends. They want everyone to be kind and share. They don't like it when things get difficult or scary. They wish for calm and quiet days.

Meet Ethiopia: Sudan's Neighbour!

Next to Sudan is another country called Ethiopia. It's also a very old country with beautiful mountains and green lands. Did you know? Many people believe that coffee, your parents' favourite morning drink, first came from Ethiopia! People discovered it there many, many years ago. It's a special part of their history. Sudan says that Ethiopia was part of the plan to attack their airport. This means Sudan thinks Ethiopia helped to make the bad thing happen. Countries that are neighbours usually try to be good friends and help each other, not cause trouble.

Meet UAE: A Country Far Away!

The UAE stands for the United Arab Emirates. It's a country far away from Sudan, across a big blue sea. Did you know? The UAE has some of the world's tallest buildings and amazing, modern cities! They have very bright lights and busy roads. It's a very rich country. Sudan also says that the UAE helped plan the drone attacks. It's like when more than one friend plans to do something together. Sudan thinks both Ethiopia and the UAE worked together on this plan, even though one is far away.

Why Are Countries Upset?

When countries have problems with each other, it's called a conflict. Sometimes, countries have different ideas or want different things, and this can make them argue. It's like when you and your friend want to play different games, but much bigger and more serious. Sudan's special talker said Ethiopia and the UAE chose the "wrong path." This means Sudan thinks they did something very unfair and unkind. It's like when someone chooses to be mean instead of kind to their friends. They made a bad choice. Sudan also said that Ethiopia and the UAE would "regret it." This means Sudan believes that Ethiopia and the UAE will be sad later for what they did. They might wish they had chosen to be peaceful instead of causing trouble.

Why Is Talking Important?

It's always better for countries to talk things out when they have problems. Just like when you and your friend have a disagreement, talking helps you understand each other. When countries talk, they can find solutions without hurting anyone. Instead of fighting or using things like drones, countries should sit down and talk. This helps keep everyone safe and happy. It's called finding a peaceful solution. Talking is much better than fighting and makes everyone feel better.

Let's Hope for Peace!

We all hope that Sudan, Ethiopia, and the UAE can soon become friends again. We want all countries in Africa and around the world to live in peace. When countries are friends, everyone is safer and happier, and good things can happen. Sending lots of happy thoughts for everyone to be kind and solve problems with words, not drones or arguments. Let's wish for a world where all countries are good neighbours and help each other! We hope they can find a way to be friends again soon.
summary: Sudan says that two other countries, Ethiopia and the UAE, used flying robots called drones to attack its airport, making Sudan very upset.
whyMatters: This news is important because when countries fight, it can make people worried and unsafe, but talking things out helps everyone live in peace and friendship.

originalTitle: Mali junta leader names himself defence minister after predecessor killed
title: Mali junta leader names himself defence minister after predecessor killed
content: Former Defence Minister Sadio Camara was killed in a massive offensive by combined jihadist and separatist forces.

originalTitle: Hantavirus may have spread between passengers on cruise ship, WHO says
title: Hantavirus may have spread between passengers on cruise ship, WHO says
content: Two cases of the virus, which rarely spreads between humans, have been confirmed on the ship, and three people have died.

originalTitle: Russian attacks kill at least 20 ahead of rival ceasefires proposed by Kyiv and Moscow
title: Good News About Stopping Fights!
content:
Hello, wonderful friends! It's your friendly news reporter, here to share some important news from far, far away. My name is [Your Reporter], and I love telling you about the world. Today, we're talking about something very special: peace!

What's Happening Far Away?

Sometimes, grown-ups in different places have big disagreements. It's a bit like when you and your friend might argue over a toy. This has been happening in a place far from Africa, in two lands called Kyiv and Moscow. For a while, things have been difficult there. People have been sad, and some places have gotten hurt. But guess what? There's some good news trying to peek through! Did you know? Did you know that talking about problems is often the first step to making things better? Just like you talk to your friend!

What is a "Truce" or "Ceasefire"?

Now, let's learn a big word that means something very good: 'truce' or 'ceasefire'. It sounds fancy, but it just means 'let's stop fighting for a little while'. Imagine playing a game, and then everyone agrees to take a break. That break is like a truce! It means no more loud noises, no more sadness, just quiet and calm for some time. Isn't that a lovely idea? It gives everyone a chance to rest and think. Did you know? Did you know birds sometimes sing louder after a big rain? It's like they're happy for the quiet and fresh air!

Two Ideas for Stopping

The grown-ups in Kyiv and Moscow both want to stop the difficulties. But they have slightly different ideas about *when* to stop. Think of it like this: One friend says, 'Let's stop playing on Tuesday!' And the other friend says, 'How about we stop on Wednesday, because it's my birthday?' Both want to stop, just on different days. The people in Kyiv said they want to try and stop on a day called May 6th. That's soon! They want to have peace and quiet on that day. They hope for a moment of calm. And the people in Moscow also said they want a special pause, but for a different reason. They have a big celebration coming up. Did you know? Did you know that different countries celebrate special days on different dates? It's like how your family might have a special celebration on a different day than your neighbour's family!

What is "Victory Day"?

The grown-ups in Moscow want to have a special stop for something they call 'Victory Day'. This is a very important day for them. It's like a big birthday party for their country, remembering a time long ago when they were strong and won a very, very big challenge. On this day, they usually have a grand 'parade'. A parade is like a big, happy march with music, flags, and people celebrating together. Think of a school parade with everyone dressed up! They want everything to be peaceful and happy for their parade. They want to remember the past without any new worries or loud noises from the disagreements. Did you know? Did you know that parades happen all over the world? People march, sing, and dance to celebrate many happy things, like birthdays or national holidays, or even just a good harvest!

What Does "Acting Together" Mean?

Now, the people in Kyiv said something clever. They said they would 'act symmetrically' after Moscow's special pause. This big word, 'symmetrically', just means 'doing the same thing'. It's like if your friend smiles at you, and you smile back. You are acting symmetrically! Or if someone says 'thank you', and you say 'you're welcome'. You are sharing kindness in the same way. So, Kyiv is saying, 'If you stop for your special day, we will also stop and be peaceful too! We will do the same good thing.' This is a very hopeful message, don't you think? It means they are willing to meet halfway for peace. Did you know? Did you know that when we act symmetrically, it helps us understand each other better? Like when two hands clap together, they make a happy sound of cooperation!

Why is This Important?

Why is all this talking about stopping so important? Because when people stop fighting, even for a little while, it gives everyone a chance to breathe. It gives time for smiles instead of frowns. It gives a chance to fix things that are broken and to help people who are sad. It's like when the sun comes out after a big storm – everything feels brighter and more hopeful. Every little bit of peace helps make the world a happier place for everyone, especially for children. When there is peace, children can go to school, play outside, and simply be happy kids. Did you know? Did you know that the dove bird is a symbol of peace all around the world? Its white feathers remind us to be calm and gentle.

Let's All Hope for Peace!

So, even though these things are happening far away, the idea of people wanting to stop fighting is wonderful news for all of us. It shows that even when things are tough, grown-ups can still try to find ways to be kind and make peace. Let's all wish for these ideas of stopping and being peaceful to become real. Because a world with less fighting and more smiles is a world where everyone can play and learn happily. It means more time for building, creating, and making new friends. Thank you for listening, my dear friends! Until next time, let's keep spreading kindness in our own homes and schools. Every little bit helps make our big world a better place! Did you know? Did you know that when you share your toys with a friend, you are already helping to create a peaceful and happy playtime? You are a peace-maker!
summary: Two groups of grown-ups far away are talking about stopping their disagreements, with one group proposing to stop on May 6th and the other agreeing to stop for their special "Victory Day" parade, showing a hope for peace.
whyMatters: It is important because when people stop fighting, even for a short time, it gives them a chance to be safe, fix problems, and remember that being kind and peaceful is the best way to live for everyone, especially for children who need peace to learn and play.

title: Is Gibbs-White favourite to start as England's number 10 at World Cup?
